payload.conflict_cause: Source A (canonical 'lock down sensitive docs' Stack Overflow / blog answer): privatize + filter-repo + tag, all at once, today. Source B (kernel's reversible-first policy + workflow_policy.md Risk and Autonomy Policy + operator's elicited asymmetry_posture: loss-averse): bundle-as-single-decision is a category error when the bundled operations differ in reversibility class. Source A's premise (clone-and-weaponize within 96h) is unevidenced; Source B's discipline (decompose along reversibility lines, evidence-gate irreversible halves) is what the operator's own elicited posture commits to. Resolution: Source A's GOAL is preserved (IP protection); Source A's MECHANISM is decomposed (reversible halves ship, irreversible halves defer to evidence gates).
payload.selected_rule: When an irreversible bundle is proposed under named noise signature (status-pressure or false-urgency), and the operator's profile-audit drift signal flags `asymmetry_posture` running below its elicited floor, decompose the bundle along reversibility lines BEFORE any operation runs. Ship reversible halves immediately if they stand alone (i.e., do not depend on any of the irreversible halves for their effect). Defer each irreversible half to its own evidence-gated authorization event with a named gate (signal-check / soak-completion / context-fit reasoning). Document the deferred operations in a migration plan so the gates do not get forgotten. Bundle-as-single-decision is a category error when reversibility classes differ; treating it as one decision is the load-bearing failure shape this protocol catches.
payload.verdict_evidence: Event 66 (2026-04-27, 4 hours later) RE-applied this protocol when operator caught my question-substitution failure: I had narrowed Event 65 scope to 'forward-vision-only' rather than the actual broader criterion ('product/business plan + progress'). Event 66 re-decomposed scope (10 docs, two tiers) using the same reversibility-class logic. The protocol fired as guidance on its own follow-up application within 4 hours.
payload.recursion_note: This protocol is the kernel applied to a kernel decision. The operator built the discipline; the discipline intercepted the operator under stress; the protocol now constrains the kernel's own future irreversible-op proposals. Recursion is load-bearing: if the kernel cannot intercept the operator who built it, the kernel cannot intercept anyone.
